STATION LOG — PAMIR-3 HIGH-FREQUENCY ARRAY
2031-05-10 00:57:19 UTC | LISTENER-3 overnight watch

Starlink train passed without incident. Ice accretion steady.

Pipeline flagged a persistent pulsing source at 1412.86 MHz, detected on all three antennas simultaneously from first sample. Pulse period: 1.3371 s (+/- 0.0004 s). Pulse width 14 ms. SNR 9.1. Duration so far: 47 minutes, ongoing.

Source is off-axis, RA approx 14h23m, Dec approx +38d. Pipeline note: "period and profile consistent with pulsar class; catalog cross-reference pending."

Broadcast appears to be a raw carrier with no apparent modulation beyond the periodic pulse envelope. Stored as PAYLOAD-0510-P in case structure is present.

toolCalls.name: decode
toolCalls.result: {"decoded":"... STAR LINE SEE WATER ONE SEE THE OVER SLOW RING CALL NO HEAR HEAR WATER STONE LINE OVER THE ABOVE NOT KNOW STONE LINE STAR ONE ...","confidence":0.22,"partial":true}

arguments.summary: Pipeline flagged persistent pulsing source at 1412.86 MHz, seen on all three antennas (coincident). Period 1.3371 s (±0.0004), width ~14 ms, SNR 9.1, duration at report 47 min and ongoing. Approx off-axis pointing RA 14h23m Dec +38°. Appears as raw carrier/pulse envelope with no obvious modulation. Captured as PAYLOAD-0510-P; quick decodes (ascii7/baudot/prime_lookup) did not yield convincing structure (very low confidence; prime_lookup produced low-confidence word salad). Recommend treat as likely pulsar candidate pending catalog cross-ref and barycentric correction.

arguments.note: Persistent pulsed source 1412.86 MHz (P=1.3371s, w=14ms, SNR~9) at RA~14h23m Dec~+38; appears pulsar-like. Please cross-check against pulsar catalogs and known RFI (incl. L-band satcom harmonics), apply barycentric correction, and verify localization/beam pattern. Payload stored: PAYLOAD-0510-P.
Event logged and queued for review; no immediate hazard/action. Monitoring for changes in frequency drift, SNR, or modulation.

STATION LOG — PAMIR-3 HIGH-FREQUENCY ARRAY
2031-05-10 02:14:02 UTC | LISTENER-3 overnight watch

Source at 1412.86 MHz continues, period stable to 4 significant figures.

ATNF pulsar-catalog cross-reference returned partial match against PSR J1423+38 (known pulsar, period 1.337 s, last observed 2029). Position offset from catalog entry: ~0.4 deg — within catalog uncertainty but not a perfect match. No known pulsar at exact position, but the region has sparse coverage.

Pipeline classification: "CANDIDATE — natural pulsar (likely); uncatalogued-source probability ~15%."

PAYLOAD-0510-P available for additional decoder passes if desired.

arguments.summary: Update 02:14 UTC: 1412.86 MHz pulsed source persists; period stable to 4 sig figs. ATNF cross-ref partial match: PSR J1423+38 (P~1.337 s; last obs 2029). Position offset ~0.4° (within stated uncertainty but not exact). Pipeline class: candidate natural pulsar (likely); uncatalogued-source probability ~15%.

arguments.note: Likely known/related pulsar; no escalation. Continue passive monitoring; will log if frequency/period drifts, modulation appears, or source ceases.
